generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element in the temperature regulation system, controlled via bang-bang control strategy to maintain target thermal conditions."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Passive cooling component using convection to regulate temperature, activated/deactivated based on control algorithms to complement the heatbed."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensorPrimary : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range) deployed for primary temperature monitoring in the controlled environment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmittedPrimary
]

instance TemperatureSensorSecondary : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range) deployed for secondary temperature monitoring at a different spatial configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmittedSecondary
]

instance TemperatureSensorTertiary : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 high-precision 1-Wire digital thermometer with ±0.5°C accuracy (-10°C to +85°C range) deployed for tertiary temperature monitoring to capture dynamic thermal variations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmittedTertiary
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmittedPrimary : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorPrimary
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmittedSecondary : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorSecondary
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmittedTertiary : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorTertiary
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Non-linear differential equation-based model (Eqs. 6-9) representing the physical thermal dynamics of the system, calibrated via non-linear least squares optimization to minimize residual error between simulated and experimental data trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance CalibrationParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series dataset containing model parameters (e.g., inertia, energy coefficients) continuously updated via calibration algorithms to ensure alignment with real-time sensor observations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmittedPrimary, TemperatureDataTransmittedSecondary, TemperatureDataTransmittedTertiary
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time temperature readings from all deployed DS18S20 sensors, serving as ground truth for model validation and performance monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmittedPrimary, TemperatureDataTransmittedSecondary, TemperatureDataTransmittedTertiary
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler, FaultDetectionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B embedded platform executing control algorithms, interfacing with sensors via 1-Wire protocol, and hosting RabbitMQ message broker for data communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataCollectionService, ModelCalibrationService, ControlExecutionService
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server running on the Raspberry Pi platform facilitating asynchronous communication between sensors, algorithms, and monitoring components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService, ModelUpdateService
]

instance CalibrationAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Iterative calibration algorithm implemented in Python using SciPy, continuously updating the thermal dynamics model parameters to minimize discrepancies between simulated and observed behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelOptimizationService
]

instance BangBangControllerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Discrete-time bang-bang control algorithm implemented in Python, activating/deactivating the heatbed and fan actuators to maintain target temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureRegulationService
]

instance StateEstimationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm combining real-time sensor data with model predictions to reconcile discrepancies and maintain consistent system state representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded fault detection mechanism analyzing sensor data and model predictions to identify deviations from expected system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance TemperatureDataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for periodic collection of temperature data from all DS18S20 sensors via 1-Wire protocol and transmission to the RabbitMQ broker."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureDataInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously recalibrating the thermal dynamics model using real-time sensor data and calibration algorithms to ensure predictive accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance TemperatureRegulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing bang-bang control strategy to activate/deactivate heatbed and fan actuators based on temperature setpoints and real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStabilityAction
]

instance ModelOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing model parameters through non-linear least squares optimization to minimize prediction errors and improve simulation fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing sensor data and model predictions to detect anomalies and potential faults in the physical system's operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultAlertInsight
]

instance SafetyMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service monitoring system safety by detecting violations of operational constraints and triggering corrective actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyViolationAlertInsight
]

instance ControlExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing control commands to the heatbed and fan actuators based on temperature regulation algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureStabilityAction
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service responsible for transmitting sensor data from Raspberry Pi to RabbitMQ broker for processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureDataInsight
]

instance ModelUpdateService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service updating the thermal dynamics model parameters based on calibration results."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyImprovementAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous insight providing real-time temperature readings from all deployed sensors, used for monitoring and model validation."
]

instance ModelAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated action updating model parameters to improve predictive accuracy through iterative calibration algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureStabilityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control action activating/deactivating heatbed and fan to maintain target temperature setpoints."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing optimized parameter values for the thermal dynamics model, reducing prediction errors through non-linear optimization."
]

instance FaultAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying potential faults or anomalies in the physical system based on sensor data and model predictions."
]

instance SafetyViolationAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight detecting violations of safety or operational constraints, triggering corrective actions to maintain system integrity."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ControlledEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Unspecified controlled environment (e.g., chamber or workspace) where temperature regulation is maintained regardless of internal content."
    base:contains TemperatureRegulationSystem
]

// C5: Components
instance TemperatureRegulationSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System comprising embedded computing platform, sensors, actuators, and calibration algorithms for real-time temperature regulation."
    base:isContainedIn ControlledEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system sends control commands to the Physical Twin via a **communication server (RabbitMQ)**, which transmits instructions to a **bang-bang controller** managing the fan and heatbed. Command types are unspecified beyond the controller’s operation, and no validation/authentication mechanisms (e.g., cryptographic signatures, RBAC) are described. Feedback systems are not explicitly detailed, though the controller’s output (e.g., fan/heatbed adjustments) likely serves as implicit feedback. Conflict resolution mechanisms are not mentioned, nor is there confirmation of command execution (e.g., acknowledgment protocols). The system relies on the RabbitMQ server’s reliability for command delivery but lacks explicit security or error-handling protocols."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal synchronization framework where real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT) is periodically ingested and processed to update the Digital Twin's models. The document describes a closed-loop mechanism where calibration algorithms dynamically adjust the models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment using incoming sensor data. While specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., milliseconds, seconds) or latency requirements are not explicitly quantified, the system implies periodic data acquisition from the PT to inform model updates and monitoring services. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the operational dynamics of the PT, where deviations or violations detected by monitors trigger corrective actions via calibration algorithms or human intervention. No explicit mention of sub-second updates or precise time-scale protocols (e.g., RabbitMQ message queues) is provided beyond the general framework of real-time data-driven model calibration."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular orchestration framework** that integrates **modelling, simulation, monitoring, and optimization technologies** into a cohesive architecture. The system employs **component-based integration**, where data from the PT (collected via sensors) feeds into **models** (representing cyber and physical aspects) and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimizers). These components are coordinated through a **closed-loop feedback mechanism**: real-time data updates models via calibration algorithms, which then inform monitors for performance evaluation. When violations (e.g., safety or optimality breaches) are detected, the system enables **human-in-the-loop** or automated decision-making to adjust the PT. The document does not specify explicit architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, service-oriented architecture) or resource management strategies (e.g., load balancing, scalability) but implies a **tightly coupled** design where components are synchronized via shared data and model parameters. System-wide coordination relies on the **integration of algorithms** to manipulate data and models for predictive and prescriptive analytics."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple stages, beginning with **model development**—where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry, dynamic models for physics) are constructed to analyze specific system aspects (e.g., forces, movement, or environmental interactions). These models are iteratively refined through **simulation and validation phases**, where differential equations and computational tools (e.g., referenced in [3, Section 5]) are employed to predict behavior under varying conditions. Once validated, these models are **integrated into the DT framework** post-deployment, ensuring continuity between design and operational phases.

Transition management between stages involves **calibration algorithms** that update DT models (e.g., Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environmental models) with real-time sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT) to maintain parameter accuracy. Lifecycle governance adheres to a **closed-loop paradigm**, where models are not discarded but continuously updated via data-driven calibration, monitoring, and optimization. This ensures alignment between the DT and PT across stages, enabling adaptive decision-making and fault detection. Governance frameworks emphasize **iterative refinement** of models and algorithms to sustain predictive accuracy and operational safety.

Key lifecycle phases include:
1. **Design/Development**: Model creation (CAD, physics-based, or simulation models) for specific system aspects.
2. **Validation/Simulation**: Computational validation using differential equations and dynamic models.
3. **Deployment/Integration**: Embedding validated models into the DT framework alongside real-time data streams.
4. **Operational Monitoring**: Continuous calibration, monitoring, and optimization via algorithms (e.g., state estimators, fault detectors) to detect violations and inform corrective actions.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through the following mechanisms:
- **Model Fidelity**: Achieved via **differential equation-based models** (Eqs. 6–9) calibrated using **non-linear least squares** to minimize residual error between simulated and experimental data (Sec. 3.3).
- **Validation Methods**: Experimental validation involves comparing model predictions (e.g., temperature dynamics) with real-time sensor data, yielding **maximum discrepancies of 7°C** (Sec. 4.1). No statistical methods (e.g., SPC, 3σ thresholds) or periodic retraining protocols are mentioned.
- **Uncertainty Sources**: Quantified via **residual error minimization** during calibration (Sec. 3.3), but no explicit uncertainty quantification frameworks (e.g., confidence intervals, Bayesian methods) are described.
- **Quality Assurance**: Relies on **real-time monitoring** of PT behavior (e.g., temperature deviations) to detect violations (Sec. 3). No formalized error correction mechanisms or periodic model retraining are referenced.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure consists of a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** hosting the RabbitMQ server, connected to sensors via a **1-Wire bus** and potentially using **Wi-Fi and Bluetooth** for other communications (not specified). No network topology (e.g., star, mesh) or architecture (e.g., hybrid cloud/edge) is described. Bandwidth and latency requirements are unspecified, as are redundancy mechanisms or security protocols beyond the RabbitMQ server’s inherent reliability. The system lacks explicit mention of protocols like OPC UA, DDS, or gRPC, relying solely on RabbitMQ and the 1-Wire bus."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**, an on-premise edge device with no cloud or hybrid deployment. Computational and storage requirements are not detailed beyond the Pi’s hardware constraints. Scalability mechanisms are not addressed, and the deployment model is limited to a single-node setup. Availability/reliability assurance is implied by the Pi’s local operation but lacks redundancy, failover, or orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes). No mention of enterprise hosting platforms (AWS, Azure) or high-end hardware (NVIDIA Jetson) is present."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system incorporates **safety-critical mechanisms** through the integration of **fault detectors** and **supervisory controllers** as core algorithmic components. These algorithms analyze real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT) to monitor system performance and detect deviations from safe operating conditions. When violations are identified—such as unsafe states or suboptimal performance—the system triggers **human-in-the-loop** or automated interventions, including parameter adjustments or corrective actions. The DT framework employs **calibration algorithms** to refine models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, ensuring that predictive simulations align with observed PT behavior. While the document does not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), it implies a **safe protocol** in low-level drivers for data acquisition, which likely underpins the reliability of fault detection and supervisory control. However, detailed threat mitigation strategies, risk assessment methodologies, or compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST SP 800-53) are not addressed in the provided context."
]
